title: buildah rename apparently not renaming
body:
**Description**
`builldah rename` doesn't seem to be renaming.

```
# buildah containers
CONTAINER ID  BUILDER  IMAGE ID     IMAGE NAME                       CONTAINER NAME
16393441df5f     *     11cd0b38bc3c docker.io/library/alpine:latest  alpine-working-container
# buildah rename alpine-working-container my-alpine

# buildah containers
CONTAINER ID  BUILDER  IMAGE ID     IMAGE NAME                       CONTAINER NAME
16393441df5f     *     11cd0b38bc3c docker.io/library/alpine:latest  alpine-working-container

```

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**
1.  buildah from alpine
2.  buildah rename alpine-working-container my-alpnie
3.  buildah containers  # No my-alpine


**Describe the results you received:**

No apparent rename happened and no error, appeared to be a no-op.

**Describe the results you expected:**
Wanted to see a container name my-alpine

**Output of `buildah version`:**

```
# buildah version 
Version:         1.4-dev
Go Version:      go1.9.7
Image Spec:      1.0.0
Runtime Spec:    1.0.0
CNI Spec:        0.3.1
libcni Version:  v0.6.0
Git Commit:      0a7389cb
Built:           Sun Aug 12 11:26:35 2018
OS/Arch:         linux/amd64
```

comments.body:
If you do:
```
# buildah containers --all
CONTAINER ID  BUILDER  IMAGE ID     IMAGE NAME                       CONTAINER NAME
36ac54d0f1fe     *     11cd0b38bc3c docker.io/library/alpine:latest  my-alpine
```
Then you see the renamed container.  However if you don't use the --all and use just the container name for buildah commands from `buildah containers`, you get an error:

```
# buildah run alpine-working-container /bin/sh
error reading build container "alpine-working-container": error reading build container: container not known

# buildah run my-alpine /bin/sh
/ # 
```

Update:  After investigating, the logic in the `buildah rename` command seems fine.  The issue is in the `buildah containers` command which is displaying the last name in the names slice rather than the one in position Zero.  Reworking.
